lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Don't suppose you've come across any strange emblems or artefacts along your journey? Ancient artefacts?

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Don't suppose you've come across any strange emblems or artefacts along your journey? Ancient artefacts, or archaic emblems - I trade in either sort.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing to report now.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: If you find any, please bring them to me. I am happy to offer rewards for such items.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's trade for rewards.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: I don't carry rewards out here, it is far too dangerous.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Meet me in Daimon's Crater if you would like to buy rewards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a PK skull, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you alone.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: I don't carry rewards out here, it is far too dangerous.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Meet me in Daimon's Crater if you would like to buy rewards.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Hello, wanderer. How may I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: This is Daimon's Crater. Once, it was home to a group of demons who set up a fortress inside this dormant volcano to mine rare minerals. However, their activity reactivated the volcano, resulting in their demise.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Years later, humans settled in the ruins. Convinced that they were the descendants of the demons, they began to hunt each other for fun.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Today, the crater is home to Bounty Hunter, a magnificent battle arena where you can engage in combat and test your mettle against skilled opponents.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I play Bounty Hunter?

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Before entering the arena, ensure that you have enough coins in your Coffer to participate. Upon entering, you will be assigned a Target to fight.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Locate your Targets and engage them in combat, defeating them to claim your reward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds easy enough.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: If you require further information about the Bounty Hunter, feel free to ask the Corrupted Warrior. They will be able to answer any questions you may have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What level Targets might I be assigned?

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: You may be assigned Targets up to [value] levels stronger than you.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Very well, you may be assigned Targets up to 5 levels stronger than you. This range only applies to Targets, as Rogue fights will always have a range of 5 from your combat level.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Very well, you may be assigned Targets up to 10 levels stronger than you. This range only applies to Targets, as Rogue fights will always have a range of 5 from your combat level.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Very well, you may be assigned Targets up to 15 levels stronger than you. This range only applies to Targets, as Rogue fights will always have a range of 5 from your combat level.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to manage my Coffer.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Do you want to add or remove coins from your coffer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a PK skull, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make my PK skull last longer?

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Sorry, I can't extend your PK skull beyond its existing duration.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do the different colour skulls mean?

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: The different colours represent risk values. Bronze is up to 200,000. Iron is up to 800,000. Green is up to 2,000,000. Blue is up to 8,000,000. Red is any amount greater than 8,000,000.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Hello, wanderer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: I used to deal in certain... emblems, for my master in the north. But now he has entrusted me with a new responsibility as the keeper of Bounty Hunter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye, trader.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: I seek certain... archaic emblems, and I believe you have some, either on your person or in your bank. I'll happily take yours off your hands for a handsome fee.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Thank you. My master in the north will be very pleased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thank you.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Let me know if you change your mind. My master is always looking to add to his collection.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: My master is pleased by your interest in emblem-hunting, so I'll mark that task off on your diary.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: What can I do for you, wanderer?

sections.title: Using an archaic emblem on him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thank you.

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: Let me know if you change your mind. My master is always looking to add to his collection.

sections.title: Requesting a skull at Grand Exchange

lines.speaker: Emblem Trader
lines.text: I don't think the guards would like me to do that in this world.
